epidemiology.diagnosticCriteria: ICHD-3: severe unilateral orbital/supraorbital/temporal pain 15-180 min with ipsilateral autonomic signs and attack frequency; distinguish from migraine and trigeminal neuralgia

publicHealthSystem.controlRate: Variable; depends on access to oxygen, triptans, and preventive therapy

regionalSpecificities.diagnosisCriteria: Often misdiagnosed; neurology access concentrated in major centers
regionalSpecificities.treatmentProtocol: Acute: high-flow oxygen and subcutaneous/intranasal triptans; prevention: verapamil and other options; avoid triggers during bouts
regionalSpecificities.regionalChallenges: Limited access to high-flow oxygen at home and specialist management; misdiagnosis delays
regionalSpecificities.strongPoints: Effective evidence-based treatments exist when diagnosis is made
